sections.paragraph:
πιέζω pass.: pf. ptc. πεπιεσμένος; 3 sg. aor. ἐπιέσθη Papias (v.l.) (Hom.+; Hero Alex. I p. 58, 4; SIG 904, 7; PCairZen 378, 11 [III b.c.]; PBerlZill 1, 41; Mi 6:15 πιέσεις ἐλαίαν; TestSol 1:2 C; Philo, Migr. Abr. 157, Aet. M. 129; Jos., Ant. 17, 28. See πιάζω) press μέτρον πεπιεσμένον a measure that is pressed down Lk 6:38. ὑπὸ τῆς ἁμάξης … ἐπιέσθη ὥστε τὰ ἔγκατα αὐτοῦ ἐκκενωθήναι Judas was so pinned by the wagon that his entrails came out Papias (3:2 v.l. [Preuschen p. 98 app. for ln. 8]).—S. Shipp, πιάζω, end. B. 575; 744. New Docs 3, 82.—DELG. M-M.

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

Papias Papias , early Christian writer, II a.d.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Hero Alex. Hero Alex. = Hero(n) of Alexandria, I? b.c./I a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PCairZen PCairZen = Zenon Papyri, Catalogue général . . . du Musée du Caire—List 4

PBerlZill PBerlZill = Vierzehn Berliner griechische Papyri, ed. HZilliacus—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

app. app. = appendix, apparatus (when ref. to marginal text-critical information, esp. in N.)

  1. ln. = line
  2. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 812
